info.name: Canon EOS 5D Mark II
info.note: Spectral response from the camspec database (Jiang et al. 2013), peak-normalized to 1.0 per channel. Trimmed from 400-720nm/10nm (33 bands) to 400-700nm/10nm (31 bands). Geometry is the native full-frame sensor (non-square 3:2). Noise derived from photon-transfer at base ISO 100. NOT the same camera as the Baek 2021 paper (which used the 5D Mark III); provided as a related/citeable Canon alternative. The HSI pipeline does not apply sensor noise by default.
info.spectral_source: camspec / Jiang et al. 2013, 'What is the Space of Spectral Sensitivity Functions for Digital Color Cameras?' (https://www.gujinwei.org/research/camspec/db.html, camspec_database.txt entry 'Canon 5DMarkII')
info.noise_source: Photon-transfer analysis by Clark Vision (https://clarkvision.com/reviews/evaluation-canon-5dii/index.html): base ISO 100 -> gain 4.1 e-/DN, read noise 23.5 e-, full well 59400 e-.
info.noise_derivation: Mapped to Sensor.simu_noise (var = alpha^2*S + read_std^2, DN at iso_base): shot_noise_std_alpha = 1/sqrt(4.1) = 0.4939; read_noise_std = 23.5/4.1 = 5.732 DN (14-bit). Valid for ISO<=400.
info.black_level_note: black_level is the libraw/dcraw-typical 14-bit value for the 5D Mark II (~1024), not measured by the noise source.
